prevalence.source: Occupational health and musculoskeletal epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; higher in manual labor and repetitive work

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant pain and work limitation

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical tendon pain with activity and focal tenderness; ultrasound or MRI if diagnosis uncertain or refractory

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Millions of cases across musculoskeletal clinics
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with physiotherapy and ergonomic changes

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care evaluation; imaging access varies by region
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Activity modification, NSAIDs, physiotherapy, ergonomic interventions; corticosteroid injections for selected cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to physiotherapy and delayed ergonomic interventions in workplaces
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective conservative management options and occupational health programs in larger employers
